Container.Name: nightlight
Container.Overview:
Nightlight is a self-hosted baby monitor with low-latency multi-camera streaming from RTSP cameras, installable as a web app (PWA) on your phone. No cloud, no subscription - runs entirely on your own network.
One container includes the app, MediaMTX (RTSP to WebRTC/HLS bridge), and FFmpeg (audio transcoding) together.
IMPORTANT: this container requires Host networking - WebRTC (the low-latency viewing mode) needs it to work correctly on your LAN. Set the data path below to somewhere under your appdata share before starting.
After starting, open the WebUI and you'll be prompted to create the first admin account.
Container.Description: Self-hosted baby monitor with low-latency multi-camera streaming (WebRTC + HLS) from RTSP cameras. No cloud, no subscription.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the web app. With Host networking this binds directly - it's listed here so Unraid can build the WebUI link above.

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Stores the database and MediaMTX's config (including your added cameras)

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID that owns files this container creates in the Data Directory - 99 matches Unraid's own 'nobody' convention, so the default should already be correct

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID that owns files this container creates in the Data Directory - 100 matches Unraid's own 'users' convention, so the default should already be correct

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Your local timezone, e.g. Australia/Melbourne - affects log timestamps only

Config.Name: JWT Secret
Config.Description: Optional - leave blank and a secure random secret is generated and persisted in the data directory automatically on first run

Config.Name: Public Host
Config.Description: Your public IP or DDNS hostname - only needed if you want Low Latency (WebRTC) mode to work from outside your home network (also requires forwarding UDP port 8189 on your router). Leave blank for LAN-only use.
